content: The intrinsic reward signals from curiosity-driven methods like ICM and RND can be repurposed as a dynamic memory pruning objective for state-of-the-art active learning agents: only unexpected, high-curiosity transitions that trigger prediction error are retained in episodic memory, while mundane, predictable observations are discarded to avoid clutter. This creates a self-organizing memory buffer that prioritizes the informative data points active learning explicitly seeks, without requiring manual memory size tuning.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
